actionUnits.condition: MonoBehaviour id 131723510 (guid a658f19182a575a46b14f720a8e295a7) is the SpawnObject script attached to 'Spawn Button'. Scene meta shows originalObject -> Box (fileID 6150933721197494150, guid 97e89670ed7696d489d6ddba4543a01b) and spawnPosition -> Transform (fileID 7863669398301968895). XRButton (MonoBehaviour id 3016069961412692236, guid acde65f5db6773e49ad668404cf55472) is also attached with UnityEvent OnPress having m_Calls to PlaySoundsFromList.PlayAtIndex (fileID 1145393436748058495) and GameObject.SetActive (fileID 41552074). To ensure spawn coverage independent of hover interactions, manually invoke SpawnObject.Spawn (public, no params). Expected: Instantiate(Box) at spawnPosition.

Box prefab has Rigidbody and BoxCollider (BoxCollider id 3174054499936259574). OnCollision (MonoBehaviour id 7040005640398465217, guid 6bf7bf2b5cedc0b4fb673b10c7900629) defines OnEnter UnityEvent with m_Calls to PlayQuickSound.Play on MonoBehaviour id 2085702217920827848 (guid 0755a2ba17a31d643b4a27e3034d8a79). To guarantee coverage of the OnEnter m_Calls without relying on a physical collision, manually trigger the PlayQuickSound.Play (public, no params).

MonoBehaviour id 1718703368 in scene meta corresponds to TranslateObject (guid 866d134ae31a01841b6156f8d0a87054) attached to GameObject 'Controls' (fileID 1718703366). Start() runs automatically to cache startPos/startRot. Since Controls has no Rigidbody and no Collider/EventTrigger listed, avoid Grab/Transform per policy. Manually invoke the only public no-parameter method to ensure coverage of the reset path: ResetToStartingPosition() sets transform back to cached start position/rotation.

MonoBehaviour id 3016069960143379342 (guid acde65f5db6773e49ad668404cf55472) corresponds to XRButton attached to 'Reset Button'. Its UnityEvent OnPress has m_Calls configured in scene meta to: (1) PlaySoundsFromList.PlayAtIndex(3) on fileID 1145393436748058495, and (2) invoke TranslateObject.ResetToStartingPosition() on multiple targets. MonoBehaviour id 1824525328 (guid 67ddb01715fbbc34fb1b56a1a852d398) corresponds to RemoveObjectsWithTag, but it exposes only a parameterized method and has no m_Calls wiring in this scene snippet. Triggering XRButton press should invoke all OnPress m_Calls within one frame.

MonoBehaviour id 5754956525560203020 in scene meta corresponds to XRLever (guid e601cf28e9702c945abe3b90e7f51974) attached to GameObject 'Lever'. This component configuration matches the provided XRLever script: handle -> fileID 7509008427246001996, defaultValue -> true, and it has a Collider (fileID 5238779549397348332), so Trigger is allowed. With defaultValue true, Start() will automatically invoke OnLeverActivate. The OnLeverActivate UnityEvent m_Calls include: SetOriginalLayer() on script fileID 7541936460893253790 (no parameters) and PlaySoundsFromList.PlayAtIndex(0) on fileID 1145393436748058495 (has parameters). To ensure coverage of the configured no-parameter event call, manually invoke SetOriginalLayer(); PlayAtIndex will be covered automatically via OnLeverActivate when Start runs.

MonoBehaviour id 774548518 in scene meta corresponds to XRKnob (guid 65a8500fa86faa04596fb5f9d40efeae) attached to GameObject 'Knob' (fileID 4502080887094448763). XRKnob defines a UnityEvent<float> (OnValueChange) and has interaction events configured: m_SelectEntered calls PlayContinuousSound.Play on fileID 4079354613688537254 and m_SelectExited calls PlayContinuousSound.Pause on the same fileID. No Rigidbody is present, so Grab/Transform are invalid. To cover the configured UnityEvent calls without simulating selection, manually invoke Play then Pause.

MonoBehaviour component id 341662333 in the scene meta uses script GUID 76a895b5e674db04d8b4f8110c068852, which corresponds to the provided PlaySoundsFromList script (MonoBehaviour) attached to GameObject 'Audio Source' (fileID 341662330).
